crypto.c(3) Library Functions Manual crypto.c(3)

NAME

crypto.c - 🔐 Server cryptography: per-client handshake, X25519 key exchange, and session encryption management

SYNOPSIS

Functions


int server_crypto_init (void)
int server_crypto_handshake (client_info_t *client)
bool crypto_server_is_ready (const char *client_id)
const crypto_context_t * crypto_server_get_context (const char *client_id)
int crypto_server_encrypt_packet (const char *client_id, const uint8_t *plaintext, size_t plaintext_len, uint8_t *ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_size, size_t *ciphertext_len)
int crypto_server_decrypt_packet (const char *client_id, const uint8_t *ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_len, uint8_t *plaintext, size_t plaintext_size, size_t *plaintext_len)
void crypto_server_cleanup_client (const char *client_id)

Detailed Description

🔐 Server cryptography: per-client handshake, X25519 key exchange, and session encryption management

1.
Initialize server crypto system and validate encryption configuration
2.
Perform cryptographic handshake with each connecting client
3.
Manage per-client crypto contexts stored in client_info_t structures
4.
Provide encryption/decryption functions for secure packet transmission
5.
Support multiple authentication modes (password, SSH key, passwordless)
6.
Integrate with client whitelist for authenticated access control

CRYPTOGRAPHIC HANDSHAKE ARCHITECTURE:

The handshake follows a multi-phase protocol:

PHASE 0: PROTOCOL NEGOTIATION:

  • Step 0a: Receive client protocol version
  • Step 0b: Send server protocol version
  • Step 0c: Receive client crypto capabilities
  • Step 0d: Select algorithms and send crypto parameters

PHASE 1: KEY EXCHANGE:

  • Step 1: Send server's ephemeral public key (X25519)
  • Server generates ephemeral key pair for this client session
  • Both sides derive shared secret using X25519 key exchange

PHASE 2: AUTHENTICATION:

  • Step 2: Receive client's public key and send auth challenge
  • Server verifies client identity (if whitelist enabled)
  • Server signs challenge with identity key (if server has identity key)
  • Step 3: Receive auth response and complete handshake

SUPPORTED AUTHENTICATION MODES:

The server supports three authentication modes:

1.
PASSWORD AUTHENTICATION:
•
Uses Argon2id key derivation from shared password
•
Both server and client derive same key from password
•
No identity keys required (password-only mode)
2.
SSH KEY AUTHENTICATION:
•
Server uses Ed25519 private key for identity verification
•
Client provides Ed25519 public key for authentication
•
Identity verification via known_hosts and whitelist
3.
PASSWORDLESS MODE:
•
Ephemeral keys only (no long-term identity)
•
Key exchange provides confidentiality but not authentication
•
Suitable for trusted networks or testing

PER-CLIENT CRYPTO CONTEXTS:

Each client has an independent crypto context stored in client_info_t:

  • crypto_handshake_ctx: Handshake state machine and cryptographic operations
  • crypto_initialized: Flag indicating handshake completion
  • Context is created during connection and cleaned up on disconnect

INTEGRATION WITH CLIENT WHITELIST:

When client whitelist is enabled:

  • Server requires client authentication during handshake
  • Client public key must be in whitelist array
  • Verification happens in crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge_socket()
  • Clients not in whitelist are rejected during handshake

ENCRYPTION/DECRYPTION OPERATIONS:

After handshake completion:

  • crypto_server_encrypt_packet(): Encrypts packets before transmission
  • crypto_server_decrypt_packet(): Decrypts received packets
  • Both functions use per-client crypto contexts
  • Automatic passthrough when encryption disabled (--no-encrypt)

ALGORITHM SUPPORT:

The server currently supports:

  • Key Exchange: X25519 (Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman)
  • Cipher: XSalsa20-Poly1305 (Authenticated Encryption)
  • Authentication: Ed25519 (when server has identity key)
  • Key Derivation: Argon2id (for password-based authentication)
  • HMAC: HMAC-SHA256 (for additional integrity protection)

ERROR HANDLING:

Handshake errors are handled gracefully:

  • Client disconnection during handshake: Log and return error
  • Protocol mismatch: Log detailed error and disconnect client
  • Authentication failure: Log and disconnect client (whitelist rejection)
  • Network errors: Detect and handle gracefully (don't crash server)
  • Invalid packets: Validate size and format before processing

THREAD SAFETY:

Crypto operations are thread-safe:

  • Each client has independent crypto context (no shared state)
  • Socket access protected by client_state_mutex
  • Per-client encryption/decryption operations are isolated
  • Global server crypto state (g_server_private_key) read-only after init

INTEGRATION WITH OTHER MODULES:

  • main.c: Calls server_crypto_init() during server startup
  • client.c: Calls server_crypto_handshake() for each new client
  • protocol.c: Uses encryption functions for secure packet transmission
  • crypto/handshake.h: Core handshake protocol implementation
  • crypto/keys/keys.h: Key management and parsing functions

WHY THIS MODULAR DESIGN:

The original server.c mixed cryptographic operations with connection management and packet processing, making it difficult to:

  • Add new authentication methods
  • Modify handshake protocol
  • Debug cryptographic issues
  • Test encryption/decryption independently

This separation provides:

  • Clear cryptographic interface
  • Easier handshake protocol evolution
  • Better error isolation
  • Improved security auditing

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

September 2025

Version

2.0 (Post-Modularization)

See also

client.c For client lifecycle management and Crypto Module context storage

crypto/handshake.h For handshake protocol implementation

crypto/keys/keys.h For key parsing and management

Definition in file src/server/crypto.c.

Function Documentation

void crypto_server_cleanup_client (const char * client_id)

Cleanup crypto resources for a specific client

Parameters

client_id Client ID to cleanup crypto for

Definition at line 749 of file src/server/crypto.c.

749                                                          {
750   client_info_t *client = find_client_by_id(client_id);
751   if (!client) {
752     return;
753   }
754 
755   if (client->crypto_initialized) {
756     crypto_handshake_destroy(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
757     client->crypto_initialized = false;
758     log_debug("Crypto handshake cleaned up for client %s", client_id);
759   }
760 }




References client_info::crypto_handshake_ctx,
    crypto_handshake_destroy(), client_info::crypto_initialized,
    find_client_by_id(), and log_debug.






int
  crypto_server_decrypt_packet (const char * client_id, const uint8_t *
  ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_len, uint8_t * plaintext, size_t
  plaintext_size, size_t * plaintext_len)


Decrypt a received packet from a specific client




Parameters


client_id Client ID that sent the packet


ciphertext Encrypted data to decrypt


ciphertext_len Length of encrypted data


plaintext Output buffer for decrypted data


plaintext_size Size of output buffer


plaintext_len Output length of decrypted data


Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure


Definition at line 733 of file
  src/server/crypto.c.


734                                                                                                    {
735   client_info_t *client = find_client_by_id(client_id);
736   if (!client) {
737     return -1;
738   }
739 
740   return crypto_decrypt_packet_or_passthrough(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, crypto_server_is_ready(client_id),
741                                               ciphertext, ciphertext_len, plaintext, plaintext_size, plaintext_len);
742 }






References crypto_decrypt_packet_or_passthrough(),
    client_info::crypto_handshake_ctx, crypto_server_is_ready(),
    and find_client_by_id().

int
  crypto_server_encrypt_packet (const char * client_id, const uint8_t *
  plaintext, size_t plaintext_len, uint8_t * ciphertext, size_t
  ciphertext_size, size_t * ciphertext_len)


Encrypt a packet for transmission to a specific client




Parameters


client_id Client ID to encrypt for


plaintext Plaintext data to encrypt


plaintext_len Length of plaintext data


ciphertext Output buffer for encrypted data


ciphertext_size Size of output buffer


ciphertext_len Output length of encrypted data


Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure

  crypto_context_t * crypto_server_get_context (const char *

Returns

bool crypto_server_is_ready (const char * client_id)


Check if crypto handshake is ready for a specific client




Parameters


client_id Client ID to check


Returns


true if encryption is ready, false otherwise


Definition at line 663 of file
  src/server/crypto.c.


663                                                    {
664   if (GET_OPTION(no_encrypt)) {
665     return false;
666   }
667 
668   client_info_t *client = find_client_by_id(client_id);
669   if (!client) {
670     return false;
671   }
672 
673   if (!client->crypto_initialized) {
674     return false;
675   }
676 
677   bool ready = crypto_handshake_is_ready(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
678   return ready;
679 }






References client_info::crypto_handshake_ctx,

int
  server_crypto_handshake (client_info_t * client)


Perform crypto handshake with client




Parameters


client Client info structure


Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure


Definition at line 181 of file
  src/server/crypto.c.


181                                                    {
182   if (!client) {
183     FATAL(ERROR_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE, "Client is NULL for crypto handshake");
184     return -1;
185   }
186 
187   // Only skip handshake if BOTH encryption and authentication are disabled
188   bool no_encrypt = GET_OPTION(no_encrypt);
189   bool no_auth = GET_OPTION(no_auth);
190 
191   if (no_encrypt && no_auth) {
192     log_debug("Crypto handshake skipped (ACIP_CRYPTO_NONE: both encryption and authentication disabled)");
193     return 0;
194   }
195 
196   // Initialize crypto context for this specific client
197   const options_t *opts = options_get();
198   const char *password = opts && opts->password[0] != '\0' ? opts->password : "";
199 
200   // Create debug name with client_id
201   char crypto_name[64];
202   SAFE_SNPRINTF(crypto_name, sizeof(crypto_name), "crypto_client_%s", client->client_id);
203 
204   int init_result;
205   if (strlen(password) > 0) {
206     // Password provided - use password-based encryption (even if SSH key is also provided)
207     log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Using password-based encryption");
208     init_result = crypto_handshake_init_with_password(crypto_name, &client->crypto_handshake_ctx, true,
209                                                       password); // true = server
210   } else {
211     // Server has SSH key - use standard initialization
212     log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Using passwordless-based encryption");
213     init_result = crypto_handshake_init(crypto_name, &client->crypto_handshake_ctx, true); // true = server
214   }
215 
216   if (init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
217     FATAL(init_result, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake for client %u", client->client_id);
218     return -1;
219   }
220   // Do NOT set crypto_initialized here - it must be set after handshake completes
221   client->crypto_initialized = false;
222 
223   // Set up server keys in the handshake context
224   if (g_server_encryption_enabled && g_server_private_key.type == KEY_TYPE_ED25519) {
225     // Copy server private key to handshake context for signing
226     memcpy(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx.server_private_key, &g_server_private_key, sizeof(private_key_t));
227 
228     // Extract Ed25519 public key from private key for identity
229     client->crypto_handshake_ctx.server_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
230     memcpy(client->crypto_handshake_ctx.server_public_key.key, g_server_private_key.public_key,
231            ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE);
232 
233     // SSH key is already configured in the handshake context above
234     // No additional setup needed - SSH keys are used only for authentication
235 
236     log_debug("Server identity keys configured for client %s", client->client_id);
237   }
238 
239   // Set up client whitelist if specified
240   if (g_num_whitelisted_clients > 0) {
241     client->crypto_handshake_ctx.require_client_auth = true;
242     client->crypto_handshake_ctx.client_whitelist = g_client_whitelist;
243     client->crypto_handshake_ctx.num_whitelisted_clients = g_num_whitelisted_clients;
244     log_info("Client whitelist enabled: %zu authorized keys", g_num_whitelisted_clients);
245   }
246 
247   // If --require-server-verify is set, require clients to provide identity keys
248   // This ensures only clients with Ed25519 identity keys can connect
249   if (GET_OPTION(require_server_verify)) {
250     client->crypto_handshake_ctx.require_client_auth = true;
251     log_info("--require-server-verify enabled: clients must provide identity keys");
252   }
253 
254   log_debug("Starting crypto handshake with client %u...", client->client_id);
255 
256   START_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
257 
258   // Step 0a: Receive client's protocol version
259   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Receiving client protocol version");
260   packet_type_t packet_type;
261   void *payload = NULL;
262   size_t payload_len = 0;
263 
264   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: About to receive packet from client %s", client->client_id);
265 
266   // Protect socket access during crypto handshake
267   mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
268   socket_t socket = client->socket;
269   mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
270 
271   if (socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
272     log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Socket is invalid for client %u", client->client_id);
273     client->crypto_initialized = false;
274     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
275     return -1;
276   }
277 
278   int result = receive_packet(socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
279   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Received packet from client %u: result=%d, type=%u", client->client_id, result,
280             packet_type);
281 
282   // Handle client disconnection gracefully
283   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
284     log_info("Client %s disconnected during crypto handshake (connection error)", client->client_id);
285     if (payload) {
286       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
287     }
288     client->crypto_initialized = false;
289     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
290     return -1; // Return error but don't crash the server
291   }
292 
293   // If client sent something other than PROTOCOL_VERSION, it means client is using --no-encrypt
294   // In this case, we need to store the packet for the caller to process and skip the handshake
295   if (packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
296     log_info("Client %u sent packet type %u instead of PROTOCOL_VERSION - using unencrypted mode", client->client_id,
297              packet_type);
298 
299     // Store this packet in the client structure so the caller can process it
300     // This is a bit of a hack, but necessary to preserve the packet we already received
301     client->pending_packet_type = packet_type;
302     client->pending_packet_payload = payload;
303     client->pending_packet_length = payload_len;
304 
305     // Mark crypto as not initialized (no encryption)
306     client->crypto_initialized = false;
307     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
308     return 0; // Success - just no encryption
309   }
310 
311   if (payload_len != sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)) {
312     log_error("Invalid protocol version packet size: %zu, expected %zu", payload_len,
313               sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
314     buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
315     client->crypto_initialized = false;
316     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
317     return -1;
318   }
319 
320   protocol_version_packet_t client_version;
321   memcpy(&client_version, payload, sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
322   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: About to free payload for client %s", client->client_id);
323   buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
324   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Payload freed for client %s", client->client_id);
325 
326   // Convert from network byte order
327   uint16_t client_proto_version = NET_TO_HOST_U16(client_version.protocol_version);
328   uint16_t client_proto_revision = NET_TO_HOST_U16(client_version.protocol_revision);
329 
330   uint8_t client_mode = client_version.supports_encryption;
331   log_info("Client %s protocol version: %u.%u (crypto mode: 0x%02x)", client->client_id, client_proto_version,
332            client_proto_revision, client_mode);
333 
334   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: About to check encryption support for client %u", client->client_id);
335 
336   // Reject ACIP_CRYPTO_NONE (plaintext only) - at minimum, ACIP handshake must run
337   if (client_mode == ACIP_CRYPTO_NONE) {
338     log_error("Client %s does not support any crypto protocol", client->client_id);
339     log_info("Client %s disconnected - crypto protocol required", client->client_id);
340     client->crypto_initialized = false;
341     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
342     return -1; // Return error but don't crash the server
343   }
344 
345   // Store encryption mode for setting encrypt_data flag later
346   client->encrypt_data = ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(client_mode);
347 
348   // Step 0b: Send our protocol version to client
349   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: About to prepare server protocol version for client %u", client->client_id);
350   protocol_version_packet_t server_version = {0};
351   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Initialized server_version struct for client %u", client->client_id);
352   server_version.protocol_version = HOST_TO_NET_U16(1);  // Protocol version 1
353   server_version.protocol_revision = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0); // Revision 0
354   server_version.supports_encryption = client_mode;      // Echo client's mode
355   server_version.compression_algorithms = 0;             // No compression for now
356   server_version.compression_threshold = 0;
357   server_version.feature_flags = 0;
358 
359   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: About to call send_protocol_version_packet for client %u", client->client_id);
360   result = send_protocol_version_packet(socket, &server_version);
361   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: send_protocol_version_packet returned %d for client %u", result,
362             client->client_id);
363   if (result != 0) {
364     log_error("Failed to send protocol version to client %u", client->client_id);
365     log_info("Client %s disconnected - failed to send protocol version", client->client_id);
366     client->crypto_initialized = false;
367     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
368     return -1; // Return error but don't crash the server
369   }
370   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Protocol version sent successfully to client %u", client->client_id);
371 
372   // Step 0c: Receive client's crypto capabilities
373   payload = NULL;
374   payload_len = 0;
375 
376   result = receive_packet(socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
377   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
378     log_info("Client %s disconnected during crypto capabilities exchange", client->client_id);
379     if (payload) {
380       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
381     }
382     client->crypto_initialized = false;
383     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
384     return -1; // Return error but don't crash the server
385   }
386 
387   if (packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CAPABILITIES) {
388     log_error("Server received packet type 0x%x (decimal %u) - Expected 0x%x (decimal %d)", packet_type, packet_type,
389               PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CAPABILITIES, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CAPABILITIES);
390     log_error("Raw packet type bytes: %02x %02x %02x %02x", (packet_type >> 0) & 0xFF, (packet_type >> 8) & 0xFF,
391               (packet_type >> 16) & 0xFF, (packet_type >> 24) & 0xFF);
392     if (payload) {
393       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
394     }
395     log_info("Client %s disconnected due to protocol mismatch in crypto capabilities", client->client_id);
396     client->crypto_initialized = false;
397     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
398     return -1; // Return error but don't crash the server
399   }
400 
401   if (payload_len != sizeof(crypto_capabilities_packet_t)) {
402     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
403               "Invalid crypto capabilities packet size: %zu (expected %zu) - we should disconnect this client",
404               payload_len, sizeof(crypto_capabilities_packet_t));
405     if (payload) {
406       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
407       payload = NULL;
408     }
409     client->crypto_initialized = false;
410     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
411     return -1;
412   }
413 
414   if (!payload) {
415     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Crypto capabilities payload is NULL after validation");
416     log_error("Client %u crypto capabilities payload is NULL after validation - disconnecting", client->client_id);
417     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Crypto handshake: missing capabilities payload");
418     client->crypto_initialized = false;
419     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
420     return -1;
421   }
422 
423   // Copy and validate payload immediately after NULL check to ensure analyzer understands safety
424   crypto_capabilities_packet_t client_caps;
425   memcpy(&client_caps, payload, sizeof(crypto_capabilities_packet_t));
426 
427   // Free the buffer after use
428   buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
429   payload = NULL; // Clear reference to prevent use-after-free
430 
431   // Convert from network byte order
432   uint16_t supported_kex = NET_TO_HOST_U16(client_caps.supported_kex_algorithms);
433   uint16_t supported_auth = NET_TO_HOST_U16(client_caps.supported_auth_algorithms);
434   uint16_t supported_cipher = NET_TO_HOST_U16(client_caps.supported_cipher_algorithms);
435 
436   log_debug("Client %s crypto capabilities: KEX=0x%04x, Auth=0x%04x, Cipher=0x%04x", client->client_id, supported_kex,
437             supported_auth, supported_cipher);
438 
439   // Step 0d: Select crypto algorithms and send parameters to client
440   crypto_parameters_packet_t server_params = {0};
441 
442   // Select algorithms (for now, we only support X25519 + Ed25519 + XSalsa20-Poly1305)
443   server_params.selected_kex = KEX_ALGO_X25519;
444   server_params.selected_cipher =
445       ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(client_mode) ? CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305 : CIPHER_ALGO_NONE;
446 
447   // Select authentication algorithm based on server configuration
448   // Note: Password authentication is not a separate algorithm - it's a mode of operation
449   // that affects key derivation. The authentication algorithm refers to signature verification.
450   //
451   // We require Ed25519 authentication if:
452   //   - Server has an identity key (g_server_encryption_enabled AND g_server_private_key is Ed25519)
453   //   - This is needed to send authenticated KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT with identity + signature
454   //   - Client whitelist verification happens during authentication phase, not key exchange
455   if (g_server_encryption_enabled && g_server_private_key.type == KEY_TYPE_ED25519) {
456     // SSH key authentication (Ed25519 signatures) - server has identity key
457     server_params.selected_auth = AUTH_ALGO_ED25519;
458   } else {
459     // No signature-based authentication during key exchange
460     // Client authentication will be required during auth phase if whitelist is enabled
461     server_params.selected_auth = AUTH_ALGO_NONE;
462   }
463 
464   // Set verification flag based on client whitelist
465   server_params.verification_enabled = (g_num_whitelisted_clients > 0) ? 1 : 0;
466 
467   // Set crypto parameters for current algorithms
468   server_params.kex_public_key_size = CRYPTO_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; // X25519 public key size
469 
470   // Only set auth/signature sizes if we're using authentication
471   if (server_params.selected_auth == AUTH_ALGO_ED25519) {
472     server_params.auth_public_key_size = ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; // Ed25519 public key size
473     server_params.signature_size = ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE;        // Ed25519 signature size
474   } else {
475     server_params.auth_public_key_size = 0; // No authentication
476     server_params.signature_size = 0;       // No signature
477   }
478 
479   server_params.shared_secret_size = CRYPTO_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; // X25519 shared secret size
480   server_params.nonce_size = CRYPTO_NONCE_SIZE;              // XSalsa20 nonce size
481   server_params.mac_size = CRYPTO_MAC_SIZE;                  // Poly1305 MAC size
482   server_params.hmac_size = CRYPTO_HMAC_SIZE;                // HMAC-SHA256 size
483 
484   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Sending crypto parameters to client %s", client->client_id);
485   result = send_crypto_parameters_packet(socket, &server_params);
486   if (result != 0) {
487     log_error("Failed to send crypto parameters to client %u", client->client_id);
488     client->crypto_initialized = false;
489     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
490     return -1;
491   }
492   log_debug("Server selected crypto for client %s: KEX=%u, Auth=%u, Cipher=%u", client->client_id,
493             server_params.selected_kex, server_params.selected_auth, server_params.selected_cipher);
494 
495   // Set the crypto parameters in the handshake context
496   result = crypto_handshake_set_parameters(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, &server_params);
497   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
498     client->crypto_initialized = false;
499     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
500     FATAL(result, "Failed to set crypto parameters for client %u", client->client_id);
501     return -1;
502   }
503 
504   // Step 0.5: Receive CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO (multi-key support)
505   // When server has multiple identity keys, client MUST send CLIENT_HELLO to select the correct key
506   // For single-key servers, CLIENT_HELLO is optional (backward compatibility)
507   if (g_num_server_identity_keys > 0) {
508     log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Waiting for optional CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO from client %u", client->client_id);
509 
510     // Try to receive CLIENT_HELLO packet
511     packet_type_t hello_packet_type;
512     uint8_t *hello_payload = NULL;
513     size_t hello_payload_len = 0;
514 
515     result = receive_packet(socket, &hello_packet_type, (void **)&hello_payload, &hello_payload_len);
516     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
517       log_info("Client %s disconnected during handshake initialization", client->client_id);
518       if (hello_payload) {
519         buffer_pool_free(NULL, hello_payload, hello_payload_len);
520       }
521       client->crypto_initialized = false;
522       STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
523       return -1;
524     }
525 
526     if (hello_packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO) {
527       // Client sent expected server key - select matching key
528       if (hello_payload_len != ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE) {
529         log_error("Invalid CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO size: %zu (expected %d)", hello_payload_len, ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE);
530         buffer_pool_free(NULL, hello_payload, hello_payload_len);
531         client->crypto_initialized = false;
532         STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
533         return -1;
534       }
535 
536       const uint8_t *expected_server_key = hello_payload;
537 
538       // Log the expected key for debugging
539       char hex_expected[65];
540       pubkey_to_hex(expected_server_key, hex_expected);
541       log_debug("Client %s expects server key: %s", client->client_id, hex_expected);
542 
543       // Search g_server_identity_keys[] for matching public key
544       bool key_found = false;
545       for (size_t i = 0; i < g_num_server_identity_keys; i++) {
546         if (sodium_memcmp(expected_server_key, g_server_identity_keys[i].public_key, ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE) == 0) {
547           // Found matching key - use this one for handshake
548           memcpy(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx.server_private_key, &g_server_identity_keys[i], sizeof(private_key_t));
549 
550           char hex_selected[65];
551           pubkey_to_hex(g_server_identity_keys[i].public_key, hex_selected);
552           log_debug("Client %s selected server identity key #%zu: %s", client->client_id, i + 1, hex_selected);
553 
554           key_found = true;
555           break;
556         }
557       }
558 
559       if (!key_found) {
560         // Client requested a key we don't have - reject connection
561         log_error("Client %s requested unknown server key %s - rejecting connection", client->client_id, hex_expected);
562         buffer_pool_free(NULL, hello_payload, hello_payload_len);
563         client->crypto_initialized = false;
564         STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
565         return -1;
566       }
567 
568       buffer_pool_free(NULL, hello_payload, hello_payload_len);
569     } else {
570       // Client didn't send CLIENT_HELLO
571       // This is ONLY acceptable for single-key servers (backward compatibility)
572       if (g_num_server_identity_keys > 1) {
573         log_error("Client %u must send CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO when server has multiple keys (got packet type %u)",
574                   client->client_id, hello_packet_type);
575         buffer_pool_free(NULL, hello_payload, hello_payload_len);
576         client->crypto_initialized = false;
577         STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
578         return -1;
579       }
580 
581       // Single-key server: Old client didn't send CLIENT_HELLO
582       // Store this packet for later processing (it's probably the first handshake packet)
583       log_debug("Client %u is using old protocol (no CLIENT_HELLO) - using default key", client->client_id);
584 
585       // Store pending packet so we can process it after KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT
586       client->pending_packet_type = hello_packet_type;
587       client->pending_packet_payload = hello_payload;
588       client->pending_packet_length = hello_payload_len;
589     }
590   }
591 
592   // Step 1: Send our public key to client
593   log_debug("About to call crypto_handshake_server_start");
594   result = crypto_handshake_server_start_socket(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, socket);
595   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
596     log_error("Crypto handshake start failed for client %u: %s", client->client_id, asciichat_error_string(result));
597     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Crypto handshake: failed to send KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT");
598     client->crypto_initialized = false;
599     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
600     return -1;
601   }
602 
603   // Step 2: Receive client's public key and send auth challenge
604   result = crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge_socket(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, socket);
605   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
606     log_error("Crypto authentication challenge failed for client %u: %s", client->client_id,
607               asciichat_error_string(result));
608     client->crypto_initialized = false;
609     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
610     return -1; // Return error to disconnect client gracefully
611   }
612 
613   // Check if handshake completed during auth challenge (no authentication needed)
614   if (client->crypto_handshake_ctx.state == CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
615     const char *cid = client->client_id;
616     client->crypto_initialized = true;
617     // Propagate encryption flag to crypto context
618     client->crypto_handshake_ctx.crypto_ctx.encrypt_data = client->encrypt_data;
619     STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(debug, 100 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "server_crypto_handshake_client_%s",
620                        "Crypto handshake completed successfully for client %s (no authentication)", cid);
621     return 0;
622   }
623 
624   // Step 3: Receive auth response and complete handshake
625   result = crypto_handshake_server_complete_socket(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, socket);
626   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
627     if (result == ERROR_NETWORK || result == ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL || result == ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH ||
628         result == ERROR_CRYPTO_VERIFICATION || result == ERROR_CRYPTO) {
629       if (result == ERROR_NETWORK) {
630         log_info("Client %s disconnected during authentication", client->client_id);
631       } else {
632         log_error("Crypto authentication failed for client %s: %s", client->client_id, asciichat_error_string(result));
633       }
634       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Crypto handshake completion failed");
635       client->crypto_initialized = false;
636       STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
637       return -1;
638     }
639     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
640     FATAL(result, "Crypto authentication response failed for client %s", client->client_id);
641     client->crypto_initialized = false;
642     return -1;
643   }
644 
645   const char *cid = client->client_id;
646   client->crypto_initialized = true;
647   // Propagate encryption flag to crypto context
648   client->crypto_handshake_ctx.crypto_ctx.encrypt_data = client->encrypt_data;
649   STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(debug, 100 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "server_crypto_handshake_client_%s",
650                      "Crypto handshake completed successfully for client %s", cid);
651 
652   // Send success notification to client (encrypted channel now established)
653   log_info_client(client, "Encryption established - secure channel ready");
654   return 0;
655 }






References ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT,
    ACIP_CRYPTO_NONE, ASCIICHAT_OK, AUTH_ALGO_ED25519,
    AUTH_ALGO_NONE,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::auth_public_key_size,
    buffer_pool_free(), CIPHER_ALGO_NONE,
    CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305, client_info::client_id,
    client_info::client_state_mutex,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_whitelist,
    protocol_version_packet_t::compression_algorithms,
    protocol_version_packet_t::compression_threshold,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx,
    client_info::crypto_handshake_ctx, crypto_handshake_init(),
    crypto_handshake_init_with_password(), CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY,
    crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge_socket(),
    crypto_handshake_server_complete_socket(),
    crypto_handshake_server_start_socket(),
    crypto_handshake_set_parameters(), CRYPTO_HMAC_SIZE,
    client_info::crypto_initialized, CRYPTO_MAC_SIZE,
    CRYPTO_NONCE_SIZE, CRYPTO_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE,
    ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE, ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE,
    client_info::encrypt_data, crypto_context_t::encrypt_data,
    ERROR_CRYPTO, ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH,
    ERROR_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE, ERROR_CRYPTO_VERIFICATION,
    ERROR_NETWORK, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, FATAL,
    protocol_version_packet_t::feature_flags, g_client_whitelist,
    g_num_server_identity_keys, g_num_whitelisted_clients,
    g_server_encryption_enabled, g_server_identity_keys,
    g_server_private_key, GET_OPTION,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::hmac_size, HOST_TO_NET_U16,
    INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, KEX_ALGO_X25519,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::kex_public_key_size,
    public_key_t::key, KEY_TYPE_ED25519, log_debug,
    LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET, log_error, log_info,
    log_info_client, crypto_parameters_packet_t::mac_size,
    mutex_lock, mutex_unlock, NET_TO_HOST_U16,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::nonce_size, NS_PER_MS_INT,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::num_whitelisted_clients,
    options_get(), PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CAPABILITIES,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO, PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION,
    options_state::password, client_info::pending_packet_length,
    client_info::pending_packet_payload,
    client_info::pending_packet_type,
    protocol_version_packet_t::protocol_revision,
    protocol_version_packet_t::protocol_version, pubkey_to_hex(),
    receive_packet(),
    crypto_handshake_context_t::require_client_auth,
    SAFE_SNPRINTF, crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_auth,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_cipher,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_kex,
    send_crypto_parameters_packet(),
    send_protocol_version_packet(),
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_private_key,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_public_key, SET_ERRNO,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::shared_secret_size,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::signature_size,
    client_info::socket, START_TIMER,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state, STOP_TIMER,
    STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::supported_auth_algorithms,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::supported_cipher_algorithms,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::supported_kex_algorithms,
    protocol_version_packet_t::supports_encryption,
    public_key_t::type, and
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::verification_enabled.


Referenced by add_client().






int
  server_crypto_init (void )


Initialize server crypto system (global initialization)




Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure


Definition at line 161 of file
  src/server/crypto.c.


161                              {
162   // Only skip handshake if BOTH encryption and authentication are disabled
163   bool no_encrypt = GET_OPTION(no_encrypt);
164   bool no_auth = GET_OPTION(no_auth);
165 
166   if (no_encrypt && no_auth) {
167     log_info("ACIP handshake disabled (both encryption and authentication disabled)");
168     return 0;
169   }
170 
171   log_debug("Server crypto system initialized (per-client contexts will be created on demand)");
172   return 0;
173 }






References GET_OPTION, log_debug, and
    log_info.


Referenced by add_client().

    Version 0.9.4
